tasks: Create template file with main() function
Include test sections for: list slicing, string formatting, exception handling
Practice typing from memory in under 5 minutes

questions.q: t=(1,2,[3]); t[2].append(4); print(t)
text='hello'; [c for c in text if c not in 'aeiou']
f = open('nonexistent.txt','r')

f = open('new.txt','x')
# file doesn't exist
f.write('data')
f.close()

What happens?

f = open('new.txt','x')

File already exists. What happens?

# file contains: hello
f = open('t.txt','r')
a = f.read(2)
b = f.read(2)
f.close()
print(a)
print(b)

What prints?

# file contains 3 lines
f = open('t.txt','r')
line1 = f.readline()
line2 = f.readline()
f.close()

What do line1 and line2 contain?
questions.a: First line (with trailing newline if present) and second line.

questions.q:
# file contains: a\nb\nc\n
f = open('t.txt','r')
lines = f.readlines()
f.close()
print(len(lines))

What prints?

What's the search path list called that Python uses to look for modules?
Where are imported modules cached? (name the dict)
If you run `import math` twice in same program, does it re-execute?
questions.a: No (it is cached; second import reuses the module object).

questions.q: What file commonly makes a directory a package in basic Python teaching?
Standard library vs third-party library?
questions.a: Stdlib ships with Python; third-party is installed separately (e.g., pip).
